title: Validate a deflate stream's internal state
notes: Faithful port of zlib's deflateStateCheck: verifies status field is one of the valid deflate modes, pending buffer is consistent, and window/hash tables are non-empty when they must be. C zlib returns 1 for invalid, 0 for valid — we invert to a Rust bool (true = valid).

title: Validate an inflate stream's internal state
notes: Faithful port of zlib's inflateStateCheck: verifies mode is a known inflate state, window/wsize coherent, and hold/bits bit-buffer invariant holds.

title: Allocation helper — returns a zeroed Vec<u8>
signature: pub fn zcalloc(opaque: usize, items: u32, size: u32) -> Vec<u8>
notes: C zlib's zcalloc calls the user's zalloc callback stored in strm->zalloc. The Rust port runs under #[forbid(unsafe_code)] and cannot honor raw C callbacks, so we own the allocation via Vec<u8>. Overflow is trapped — if items*size wraps, we return an empty Vec (caller must check length).

title: Free helper — releases ownership by consuming the Vec
signature: pub fn zcfree(opaque: usize, ptr: Vec<u8>)
notes: In C zlib, zcfree calls the user's zfree callback. In our safe-Rust port, taking Vec<u8> by value drops it at scope end — the allocation is released deterministically. opaque is unused for symmetry with the C API.

title: Bit vector describing compile-time type sizes and feature flags
notes: Mirrors zlib's zlibCompileFlags (zutil.c). Reports sizes of uInt (4), uLong (8 on 64-bit), pointers (8 on 64-bit), off_t (8). Bits 10-13 are free. Bit 16: DEBUG option. Bits 24-27: compiler info (we report Rust as 3=unknown). We compute sizes at compile time for the actual target, matching C's sizeof()-driven logic.

title: Inject bits into the inflate bit buffer
signature: pub fn inflatePrime(strm: &mut InflateStream, bits: i32, value: i32) -> Result<(), InflateError>
notes: Faithful port of zlib's inflatePrime. Valid ranges strictly enforced per C spec: bits in [-1, 16]. bits == -1 resets the buffer. Any other out-of-range value is an error. The buffer itself is 64-bit (strm.state.hold: u64) and cannot exceed 64 bits used.

title: Copy the deflate dictionary out of the state
signature: pub fn deflateGetDictionary(strm: &DeflateStream, dictionary: &mut [u8], dictLength: &mut usize) -> Result<(), DeflateError>
notes: Faithful port of zlib's deflateGetDictionary. Copies the lesser of dictionary.len() and the actual dictionary length. dictLength receives the actual length (not the amount copied) so callers can detect truncation.

title: Preload a dictionary into the deflate state
signature: pub fn deflateSetDictionary(strm: &mut DeflateStream, dictionary: &[u8]) -> Result<(), DeflateError>
notes: Faithful port: loads `dictionary` into the sliding window (truncating to the last wsize bytes if longer), seeds the adler checksum via the crate's adler32 primitive, and rehashes the dictionary content to pre-populate the match chains.
